description_long: This is a set of three interconnected round tables of varying heights and diameters, arranged in a cluster. The largest table features a gray, concrete-like surface with a slightly mottled texture, supported by four black metal legs. The medium table has a brown, stone-textured top and is also supported by four black legs, positioned slightly lower and to the side of the largest table. The smallest table has a woven, straw-like surface in a light tan color, also with four black legs, and is nestled closely to the other two. The tables are visually distinct in their surface materials, creating a modern, eclectic look suitable for urban environments. The overall design is minimalist, with no visible decorative elements or attached seating. The arrangement allows for flexible use as a coffee table cluster or as individual side tables.
description: A cluster of three round tables with distinct surfaces—gray concrete, brown stone, and tan woven—supported by black legs, forming a modern, minimalist set.
description_view_0: The largest gray table dominates the foreground, with the brown and tan tables partially visible behind and to the right.
description_view_1: All three tables are clearly visible, with the tan woven table in front, the brown stone table to the right, and the gray concrete table behind.
description_view_2: The gray concrete table is prominent on the right, with the brown and tan tables receding to the left and partially obscured.
description_view_3: The gray concrete table is centered and closest, with the brown and tan tables mostly hidden behind it.
